stemHtml:
Refer to figure.
One of the tasks that must be completed prior to commencing a flight is to have an awareness of what instrument approach procedures are available at the destination aerodrome and any alternates. The annex shows an example of an RNP approaches to an aerodrome (only the top section of the approach plate is required). The information that is highlighted in red means that…

options.a: ILS approaches augmented by GNSS are available.
options.b: GLS approaches augmented by GBAS are available.
options.c: RNAV approaches augmented by GBAS are available.
options.d: GNSS approaches augmented by SBAS are available.
